title: French Omelette
summary: The best way to cook an egg. A dead simple recipe that everyone needs to master.

method.description: Crack your eggs into a bowl and beat them up with a fork. Finely chop your chives and beat them through the eggs.

Melt ½ the butter in a non-stick frying pan set over a medium heat. Once the butter is bubbling and foaming, pour in the eggs and use a spatula to keep the eggs moving around the pan until just starting to solidify in places, then stop touching them and leave them alone until almost cooked through.
Season generously with sea salt, then use your spatula to roll the omelette up and flip it out onto a plate. Brush with the remaining butter and sprinkle over some extra sea salt.

chefs.name: Ben Lippett
chefs.summary: Ben Lippett (@dinnerbyben) is a cook, food writer, recipe developer, and co-founder of Dr Sting's Hot Honey. Ben spent years working at a spate of restaurants in the UK and Australia – including a brief but rewarding stint at a butcher's – before making the leap to food media.
